name: Mega Venusaur
abilityEffect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes from Fire- and Ice-type moves.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Confirmed in Reg M-A newly useable list.

name: Mega Charizard X
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's contact moves by 30%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Two-form Charizard.

name: Mega Charizard Y
abilityEffect: Summons harsh sunlight for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Two-form Charizard.

name: Mega Blastoise
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's pulse moves by 50%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Beedrill
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of moves of the same type as the Pokémon by 100% instead of 50%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Pidgeot
abilityEffect: The accuracy of moves used both by and against the Pokémon becomes 100%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Clefable
abilityEffect: Instead of being affected by other Pokémon's status moves, the Pokémon bounces them back at the user.
notes: New Champions Mega. Serebii confirms as first appearance in traditional battle system.

name: Mega Alakazam
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it changes its Ability to match that of an opponent.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Victreebel
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move that knocks it out, it deals the same amount of damage to the attacker.
notes: New Champions Mega.

name: Mega Slowbro
abilityEffect: Attacks landed on the Pokémon will never be critical hits.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Stone has no effect on Galarian Slowbro per item description.

name: Mega Gengar
abilityEffect: Opponents cannot be switched out of battle.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Kangaskhan
abilityEffect: The parent and child attack one after the other. The power of the child's attacks is 1/4 of those of the parent.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Child attack at 1/4 power (Champions update from Gen 6's 1/2).

name: Mega Starmie
abilityEffect: Doubles the power of the Pokémon's physical moves.
notes: New Champions Mega. Total listed as 660 in one Game8 snippet, 620 in stat breakdown — using stat sum as authoritative (620). CONFLICTING TOTAL: see audit report.

name: Mega Pinsir
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Flying-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Gyarados
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the Ability of the target (with certain exceptions).
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Aerodactyl
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's contact moves by 30%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Dragonite
abilityEffect: Halves the damage the Pokémon takes while its HP is full.
notes: New Champions Mega (first introduced in Legends: Z-A). ATK stat is reduced from base Dragonite 134 → 124 (Mega shifts to SpA focus). Confirmed Dragon/Flying type.

name: Mega Meganium
abilityEffect: Even when the sunlight has not turned harsh, the Pokémon can use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight.
megaStoneSource: Shop: 2000 VP; Battle Pass: Season M-1
notes: New Champions Mega. Type changes to Grass/Fairy. New ability Mega Sol confirmed by Serebii.

name: Mega Feraligatr
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
megaStoneSource: Shop: 2000 VP; Battle Pass: Season M-1
notes: New Champions Mega. Type changes to Water/Dragon. New ability Dragonize confirmed by Serebii.

name: Mega Ampharos
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the Ability of the target (with certain exceptions).
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Steelix
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves by 30% in a sandstorm.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Scizor
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's moves by 50% if their power is 60 or less.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Heracross
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's multistrike moves always hit the maximum number of times.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Skarmory
abilityEffect: The Pokémon ignores the effects of Abilities and moves that draw in moves.
notes: New Champions Mega. Steel/Flying type confirmed.

name: Mega Houndoom
abilityEffect: In harsh sunlight, the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat is boosted by 50%, but it loses 1/8 of its max HP at the end of every turn.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Tyranitar
abilityEffect: Summons a sandstorm for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Gardevoir
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Fairy-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Sableye
abilityEffect: Instead of being affected by other Pokémon's status moves, the Pokémon bounces them back at the user.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Aggron
abilityEffect: Reduces the damage the Pokémon takes from super-effective moves by 25%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Loses Rock type, becomes pure Steel. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Medicham
abilityEffect: Doubles the power of the Pokémon's physical moves.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Manectric
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it lowers the Attack stats of opponents by 1 stage.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Sharpedo
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's biting moves by 50%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Camerupt
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's moves lose their additional effects, but the power of those moves will be boosted by 30%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Altaria
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Fairy-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Banette
abilityEffect: Increases the priority of the Pokémon's status moves by 1 stage.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Chimecho
abilityEffect: The Pokémon floats off the ground, making it immune to Ground-type moves, as well as the Spikes, Toxic Spikes, and Sticky Web statuses.
notes: New Champions Mega. Gains Steel secondary type per Destructoid. Levitate provides Ground immunity despite Steel typing.

name: Mega Absol
abilityEffect: Instead of being affected by other Pokémon's status moves, the Pokémon bounces them back at the user.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Glalie
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Ice-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Lopunny
abilityEffect: The Pokémon can hit Ghost types with Normal- and Fighting-type moves. It is also unaffected by Intimidate.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Garchomp
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves by 30% in a sandstorm.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Lucario
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of moves of the same type as the Pokémon by 100% instead of 50%.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Abomasnow
abilityEffect: Summons snow for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega. Tutorial reward stone.

name: Mega Gallade
abilityEffect: The Pokémon never flinches when attacked and is unaffected by Intimidate.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Froslass
abilityEffect: Summons snow for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle.
notes: New Champions Mega. Aurora Veil setter archetype. Snow from Snow Warning boosts Ice-type moves and enables Aurora Veil.

name: Mega Emboar
abilityEffect: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the Ability of the target (with certain exceptions).
megaStoneSource: Shop: 2000 VP; Battle Pass: Season M-1
notes: New Champions Mega. Fire/Fighting type retained.

name: Mega Excadrill
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.
notes: New Champions Mega. New ability Piercing Drill is functionally identical to the Champions-nerfed Unseen Fist but applies to all contact moves (not just punching).

name: Mega Audino
abilityEffect: The Pokémon has a 50% chance of curing the status conditions of its allies at the end of every turn.
notes: Legacy Gen 6 Mega.

name: Mega Chandelure
abilityEffect: When using its moves, the Pokémon ignores the effects of targets' Light Screen, Reflect, Aurora Veil, Safeguard, and substitutes.
notes: New Champions Mega.

name: Mega Golurk
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.
notes: New Champions Mega. Uses the UPDATED Unseen Fist (25% through Protect, not 100%). Piercing Drill on Excadrill is a renamed version of the same mechanic.

name: Mega Chesnaught
abilityEffect: The Pokémon is immune to ball and bomb moves.
megaStoneSource: Transfer Gifts: Transfer Chesnaught to Pokemon Champions
notes: New Champions Mega. Transfer Gift — requires Chesnaught transfer via HOME.

name: Mega Delphox
abilityEffect: The Pokémon floats off the ground, making it immune to Ground-type moves, as well as the Spikes, Toxic Spikes, and Sticky Web statuses.
megaStoneSource: Transfer Gifts: Transfer Delphox to Pokemon Champions
notes: New Champions Mega. Transfer Gift — requires Delphox transfer via HOME.

name: Mega Greninja
abilityEffect: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once per time the Pokémon enters battle.
megaStoneSource: Transfer Gifts: Transfer Greninja to Pokemon Champions
notes: New Champions Mega. Transfer Gift — requires Greninja transfer via HOME. Protean triggers once per entry per Champions rules.

name: Mega Floette
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Fairy-type moves of all Pokémon on the field by 33%.
megaStoneSource: Transfer Gifts: Transfer Eternal Flower Floette to Pokemon Champions
notes: New Champions Mega. Requires Eternal Flower Floette specifically (rare event Pokémon). Transfer Gift only.

name: Mega Meowstic (Male)
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it changes its Ability to match that of an opponent.
notes: New Champions Mega. Male form has higher SpA. One Mega Stone for both forms.

name: Mega Meowstic (Female)
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it changes its Ability to match that of an opponent.
notes: New Champions Mega. Female form has same stats as base form per Game8 — female Mega stat total is identical to base female (466). Possible DATA GAP: Female Mega may not differ from base — see audit.

name: Mega Hawlucha
abilityEffect: The accuracy of moves used both by and against the Pokémon becomes 100%.
notes: New Champions Mega.

name: Mega Crabominable
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of the Pokémon's punching moves by 20%.
notes: New Champions Mega.

name: Mega Drampa
abilityEffect: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat by 1 stage when an attack causes its HP to drop to 1/2 or less of its max.
notes: New Champions Mega. Trick Room archetype. Type remains Normal/Dragon.

name: Mega Scovillain
abilityEffect: When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, it burns the attacker.
notes: New Champions Mega. New ability Spicy Spray confirmed by Serebii.

name: Mega Glimmora
abilityEffect: Boosts the power of moves of the same type as the Pokémon by 100% instead of 50%.
notes: New Champions Mega.
